# US-001: Auto-Generate AI Discharge Summary Within 30 Seconds

> **Epic:** EP-004 — Documentation Agent | **Sprint:** 2 | **Points:** 8 | **Priority:** Must Have

## User Story

**As an** attending physician,
**I want** a draft discharge summary automatically generated within 30 seconds of a discharge event,
**so that** I spend time reviewing and approving — not writing from scratch — reducing documentation time from 2 hours to under 30 minutes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC-1:** Documentation Agent subscribes to `docs-agent-sub`; on A03 event, fetches Patient, Encounter, Condition (ICD-10 codes), and MedicationStatement from FHIR via EP-002 client
- [ ] **AC-2:** LangChain orchestrator calls Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro with streaming enabled; structured JSON output validated against `DischargeSummarySchema` (Pydantic model): required fields = `primary_diagnosis`, `hospital_course`, `medications_at_discharge`, `follow_up_instructions`, `warning_signs`
- [ ] **AC-3:** Document generated within 30 seconds for 95% of test encounters (measured from A03 Pub/Sub delivery to `Document.status=PENDING_REVIEW`)
- [ ] **AC-4:** If Gemini call times out at 25 seconds: template-based fallback generates skeleton summary with patient-specific fields; `Document.generation_type=TEMPLATE`; physician notified to complete manually
- [ ] **AC-5:** Generated `Document` record: `status=PENDING_REVIEW`, `ai_assisted_label=true`, content encrypted at rest (EP-DATA US-002)
- [ ] **AC-6:** Physician approval queue on dashboard updated via SignalR push within 1 second of document creation

## Definition of Done

- [ ] 30-second SLA verified in staging with real Vertex AI calls (not mocked)
- [ ] Physician UAT: ≥80% of test summaries rated clinically acceptable without major revision
- [ ] Template fallback tested and produces a usable skeleton document

## Dependencies

- EP-002 US-001 (FHIR client)
- EP-003 US-001 (AgentTask framework)
- EP-DATA US-001 (Document ORM model)
